1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Project Overview
     6→
     7→**BeFirst Portal** is an ASP.NET MVC 5 manufacturing portal (.NET Framework 4.8) for tracking production, quality, materials, dashboards, and other factory floor operations. It integrates heavily with the **PLEX ERP** system via SOAP APIs.
     8→
     9→- **Solution**: `Portal_BeFirst.sln`
    10→- **Main project**: `firstronic/Portal_BeFirst.csproj`
    11→- **Assembly/Namespace**: `Firstronic`
    12→- **Source control**: GitLab (`gitlab.lcx.app`)
    13→
    14→## Build & Run
    15→
    16→This is a classic .NET Framework 4.8 project. It does **not** support `dotnet build` or `dotnet run`.
    17→
    18→```bash
    19→# Build via MSBuild (Visual Studio Developer Command Prompt)
    20→msbuild firstronic/Portal_BeFirst.csproj /p:Configuration=Debug
    21→
    22→# Or open Portal_BeFirst.sln in Visual Studio and build (F5 to run with IIS Express)
    23→```
    24→
    25→There are no unit tests in this project. There is no linting or formatting tool configured.
    26→
    27→## Required Environment Variables
    28→
    29→All configuration is via environment variables (nothing in Web.config). These must be set before running:
    30→
    31→| Variable | Purpose |
    32→|----------|---------|
    33→| `BeFirst:ConnectionString` | Main SQL Server database |
    34→| `BeFirst:ConnectionString_STG` | Staging database |
    35→| `BeFirst:ConnectionString_ODS` | Operational data store |
    36→| `BeFirst:ConnectionString_DWH` | Data warehouse |
    37→| `BeFirst:ConnectionStringMachines` | Machine data |
    38→| `BeFirst:ConnectionStringHanwha` | Hanwha SMT machines |
    39→| `BeFirst:PLEXSOAPUsername/Password` | PLEX ERP SOAP credentials |
    40→| `BeFirst:APIUrl/APIUser/APIPassword` | Internal API |
    41→| `BeFirst:SMTPEmail/SMTPPassword` | Email sending |
    42→| `BeFirst:FilesIP` | Network file storage |
    43→
    44→Full list in `firstronic/Framework/GlobalVariables/GlobalVariables.cs`.
    45→
    46→## Architecture
    47→
    48→### Areas Pattern (Primary Organization)
    49→
    50→The app is organized into **18 MVC Areas** under `firstronic/Areas/`. Each area has its own `Controllers/`, `Models/`, `Views/`, and `{Name}AreaRegistration.cs`:
    51→
    52→| Area | Domain |
    53→|------|--------|
    54→| **Dashboards** | TV displays, 3D floor layout (Three.js), OEE, downtime, packout charts |
    55→| **Production** | Packout goals, shortages, rework, reports, PLEX transaction reconciliation |
    56→| **Quality** | CAPAS audits, ESD audits, 5S audits, first piece inspection, defects, scrap |
    57→| **Materials** | Warehouse ops, kanban, tool crib, MSL reset, receiving, shortages |
    58→| **Engineering** | Part numbers, setups, rates, tools, feeders, stencils |
    59→| **Tracking** | Unit history, container movements, BOM used, serial tracking, road map |
    60→| **Manufacturing** | OEE, SMT reports (SPI, AOI, pick & place), solder paste |
    61→| **Manage** | Stations, areas, cells, users, roles, settings, agents, extractors |
    62→| **Planning** | Production planning tool, balance, job hints |
    63→| **Tickets** | Internal ticketing system with dashboards |
    64→| **Personnel** | Teams/bonus, visitors, QA user locks |
    65→| **Mobile** | Mobile-optimized interfaces (SMT) |
    66→| **Rework** | Rework tracking |
    67→| **Testing** | Test operations |
    68→| **Purchasing** | Procurement |
    69→| **IS/IT** | Information systems, trainings |
    70→| **Utils** | Printers |
    71→
    72→### Framework Layer (`firstronic/Framework/`)
    73→
    74→Custom infrastructure providing cross-cutting concerns:
    75→
    76→- **`BaseController.cs`** - All controllers inherit from this. Provides language/culture selection (cookie-based), notification helpers (Success/Error/Info/Warning), error handling
    77→- **`BePlex.cs`** - Large (480KB) PLEX ERP SOAP integration. Production data, BOMs, work orders
    78→- **`Data/`** - Dual database context approach:
    79→  - `LegacyDataContext/DataContext.cs` - EF6 `IdentityDbContext` for ASP.NET Identity and legacy tables
    80→  - `AppDataContext/AppDbContext.cs` - EF Core 3.1 for newer features (PLEX maintenance data)
    81→  - `Repository.cs` / `IRepository.cs` - Generic repository pattern over EF6
    82→- **`Functions/Queries.cs`** - Raw ADO.NET SQL helpers (`GetDatatableFromQuery()`, `GetIntFromQuery()`)
    83→- **`GlobalVariables/GlobalVariables.cs`** - All environment variable configuration
    84→- **`Account/`** - ASP.NET Identity 2.0 (`ApplicationUser` with Badge, ControlID, Location fields)
    85→- **`UI/`** - Custom HTML helpers (`HtmlHelper.Fst()` extensions), `ViewComponentBase`
    86→- **`Cache/`** - `MemoryCacheManager` / `ICacheManager` (singleton)
    87→- **`Languages/`** - Localization via `Settings.resx` (English/Spanish)
    88→- **`APIs/`** - Internal API clients (Station, Materials, Containers, Account)
    89→
    90→### Dependency Injection
    91→
    92→Uses **Autofac** (configured in `App_Start/DependencyRegister.cs`):
    93→- `DataContext` as `IDataContext` (per-request)
    94→- `AppDbContext` (per-request)
    95→- `MemoryCacheManager` as `ICacheManager` (singleton)
    96→- `ApplicationUserService` for Identity
    97→- `Engine.cs` provides service locator via `Engine.Resolve<T>()`
    98→
    99→### Authentication
   100→
   101→Cookie-based via OWIN middleware (`App_Start/Startup.Auth.cs`). ASP.NET Identity 2.0 with custom `ApplicationUser` extending `IdentityUser`.
   102→
   103→### Frontend
   104→
   105→- **Razor views** (~1800 `.cshtml` files) with layouts:
   106→  - `_Layout.cshtml` - Main layout
   107→  - `_Layout_portal.cshtml` - Portal layout
   108→  - `_Layout_tvs_*.cshtml` - TV dashboard layouts (color variants)
   109→- **CSS/JS bundling** via `BundleConfig.cs`
   110→- **Key libraries**: Bootstrap 5.3, jQuery 3.7, Font Awesome 6, Chart.js, DataTables, Select2, TinyMCE, Toastr, Three.js (3D factory layout), D3.js
   111→- Static assets in `firstronic/Content/` (css, img, vendor libraries)
   112→
   113→### Navigation
   114→
   115→Menu structure is built dynamically in `Models/Menus/MenuIndex.cs` using database queries for areas/stations. Uses custom `Nav`/`Dropdown` classes from `Framework/UI/`.
   116→
   117→## Key Conventions
   118→
   119→- Controllers use raw SQL via `Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()` for read-heavy operations and the `Repository<T>` pattern for CRUD
   120→- Localization strings are in `Framework/Languages/Settings.resx` (accessed via `Resources.Menu.*`, `Resources.Settings.*`)
   121→- New services should be registered in `App_Start/DependencyRegister.cs`
   122→- Database entities extend `Framework/Entities/BaseEntity`
   123→- File uploads go to `firstronic/ExcelUploads/` and `firstronic/UploadedFiles/`
   124→- Session timeout is 60 minutes (Web.config)
   125→- Max upload size is 1GB (Web.config `maxRequestLength`)
   126→
   127→## Git Workflow
   128→
   129→- **Primary branch**: `develop` (active development)
   130→- **Main branch**: `master`
   131→- Commit message prefixes used: `STYLE:`, `DELETE:`, `FIX`, `Fix:`
   132→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text:
Now let me compile a comprehensive summary. I have all the information I need. Let me create the report:

## BeFirst Portal - User, Role, Permission, and Position Data Model

I've completed a thorough exploration of the BeFirst_Portal codebase. Here's the comprehensive analysis:

### 1. **User Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationUser.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`ApplicationUser`** (extends `IdentityUser` from ASP.NET Identity 2.0)
  - Inherits from Microsoft.AspNet.Identity - base Identity properties (Id, UserName, Email, etc.)
  - Custom properties:
    - `string Name` (max 128 chars)
    - `string Surname` (max 128 chars)
    - `string NormalizedUserName`
    - `string NormalizedEmail`
    - `string Description`
    - `string Location`
    - `string Badge` (max 128 chars, display name for badge readers)
    - `int? PictureId`
    - `bool IsActive`
    - `int? ControlID`

- **`ApplicationUserModel`** (ViewModel for UI)
  - Mirrors `ApplicationUser` with additional properties: `IsAdmin`, `ControlID`, `AvatarId`, `AvatarUrl`
  - Contains `string[] SelectedRoleNames` and `List<ApplicationRoleModel> Roles` for role assignment

---

### 2. **Role Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationRole.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`tbl_AspNetRoles`** (maps to `AspNetRoles` table)
  - `string Id` (primary key)
  - `string Name`
  - `int? Facility_Key` (facility-specific roles)
  - `bool Parent` (role hierarchy flag)
  - `IEnumerable<SelectListItem> FacilityList` (UI dropdown)

- **`tbl_AspNetRoles_FULL`** (extended view model)
  - Extends `tbl_AspNetRoles`
  - `string Facility_Name`
  - `IEnumerable<SelectListItem> Parent_List`
  - `int UserCount` (count of users with this role)
  - `string rolid`

- **`ApplicationRoleModel`** (ViewModel)
  - `string Id`
  - `bool Parent`
  - `string Name` (required)
  - `int UserCount`

- **`ApplicationRole`** (extends `IdentityUserRole`)
  - Minimal - primarily used for ASP.NET Identity integration

**Role Controller:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\RoleController.cs`
- Requires `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]`
- Uses raw SQL queries to fetch roles with user counts
- Example role names in code: `"BF Administrator"`, `"BF Manage User Password"`, `"BF User Manager (Training)"`, `"BF User Position Manage"`, `"BF User Position Add"`

---

### 3. **Permission Model/Entity Classes**

**Important Finding:** BeFirst Portal uses **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** via ASP.NET Identity. **There is NO explicit "Permission" entity/table.**

Instead:
- Permissions are managed implicitly through **roles** and **[Authorize(Roles="...")] attributes** on controller actions
- Example: `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF User Position Manage")]` on the `PositionController.Index()` action
- Role names ARE the permissions (e.g., "BF User Position Add" is both a role and a permission)

**Authorization Pattern:**
- Uses `System.Web.Mvc` `[Authorize]` attribute (MVC5)
- Role-based authorization: `[Authorize(Roles = "RoleName1,RoleName2")]`
- Claims-based identity available via `ClaimsIdentity` in `ApplicationUser.GenerateUserIdentityAsync()`

---

### 4. **Position Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPosition.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`tbl_AspNetPosition`** (maps to `AspNetPosition` table)
  - `int PositionID` (primary key)
  - `string Position` (job title/position name)

- **`tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL`** (extended view model)
  - Extends `tbl_AspNetPosition`
  - No additional properties (currently empty extension)

- **`tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL_partial`** (partial ViewModel)
  - `IQueryable<tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL> Items`
  - `int id`

**Position Controller:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\PositionController.cs`
- Requires `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF User Position Manage,BF User Position Add")]`
- Uses raw SQL: `INSERT INTO [dbo].[AspNetPosition] (Position) VALUES (@Position)`
- Example positions stored as simple names like "Manager", "Technician", etc.

---

### 5. **Entity Relationships**

**Database Tables/Mappings:**

| Table | Maps To | Primary Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `AspNetUsers` | `ApplicationUser` | User accounts (ASP.NET Identity) |
| `AspNetRoles` | `ApplicationRole` (via `tbl_AspNetRoles`) | Role definitions |
| `AspNetUserRoles` | `tbl_AspNetUserRoles` | User ↔ Role mapping |
| `AspNetPosition` | `tbl_AspNetPosition` | Job positions |
| `AspNetUserPositions` | `tbl_AspNetUserPositions` | User ↔ Position mapping |
| `AspNetPositionRoles` | `tbl_AspNetPositionRoles` | Position ↔ Role mapping |

**Relationship Diagram:**
```
ApplicationUser (1) -------- (*) AspNetUserRoles (many) -------- (1) ApplicationRole
    Id                        UserId/RoleId                        Id

ApplicationUser (1) -------- (*) AspNetUserPositions (many) -------- (1) Position
    Id                        UserId/PositionId                     PositionID

Position (1) -------- (*) AspNetPositionRoles (many) -------- (1) ApplicationRole
    PositionID               PositionID/RoleID                      Id
```

---

### 6. **Key Relationship Classes**

**User-Role Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationRoleUsers.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetUserRoles")]
public class tbl_AspNetUserRoles
{
    public string UserId { get; set; }
    public string RoleId { get; set; }
    public int? Facility_Key { get; set; }  // Multi-facility support
}
```

**User-Position Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPositionUsers.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetUserPositions")]
public class tbl_AspNetUserPositions
{
    public int AutoId { get; set; }
    public string UserId { get; set; }
    public int? PositionId { get; set; }
}
```

**Position-Role Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPositionRoles.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetPositionRoles")]
public class tbl_AspNetPositionRoles
{
    public int AutoID { get; set; }
    public int? PositionID { get; set; }
    public string RolID { get; set; }  // Note: typo in "RolID"
}
```

---

### 7. **Existing Queries and Services**

**Service Layer:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationUserService.cs`
- Extends `UserManager<ApplicationUser>`
- Provides: `GetById(userId)`, `GetByUserName(userName)`
- Uses `IUserStore<ApplicationUser>` for data access
- Configured via Autofac in `App_Start/DependencyRegister.cs`

**Query Examples from Controllers:**

**Role retrieval with user counts** (RoleController.Index):
```sql
SELECT [Id], [Name], [Facility_Key], [Parent], Count(AspNetUserRoles.UserId) AS UserCount
FROM AspNetRoles 
LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id
WHERE IsVisible=1 
GROUP BY [Id], [Name], [Facility_Key], [Parent]
ORDER BY Name ASC
```

**Position retrieval** (PositionController.Index):
```sql
SELECT [PositionID], [Position] FROM [AspNetPosition] ORDER BY Position
```

---

### 8. **Database Context**

**Primary Context:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Data\LegacyDataContext\DataContext.cs`
- Extends `IdentityDbContext<ApplicationUser>` (EF6)

Now let me compile a comprehensive summary. I have all the information I need. Let me create the report:

## BeFirst Portal - User, Role, Permission, and Position Data Model

I've completed a thorough exploration of the BeFirst_Portal codebase. Here's the comprehensive analysis:

### 1. **User Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationUser.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`ApplicationUser`** (extends `IdentityUser` from ASP.NET Identity 2.0)
  - Inherits from Microsoft.AspNet.Identity - base Identity properties (Id, UserName, Email, etc.)
  - Custom properties:
    - `string Name` (max 128 chars)
    - `string Surname` (max 128 chars)
    - `string NormalizedUserName`
    - `string NormalizedEmail`
    - `string Description`
    - `string Location`
    - `string Badge` (max 128 chars, display name for badge readers)
    - `int? PictureId`
    - `bool IsActive`
    - `int? ControlID`

- **`ApplicationUserModel`** (ViewModel for UI)
  - Mirrors `ApplicationUser` with additional properties: `IsAdmin`, `ControlID`, `AvatarId`, `AvatarUrl`
  - Contains `string[] SelectedRoleNames` and `List<ApplicationRoleModel> Roles` for role assignment

---

### 2. **Role Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationRole.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`tbl_AspNetRoles`** (maps to `AspNetRoles` table)
  - `string Id` (primary key)
  - `string Name`
  - `int? Facility_Key` (facility-specific roles)
  - `bool Parent` (role hierarchy flag)
  - `IEnumerable<SelectListItem> FacilityList` (UI dropdown)

- **`tbl_AspNetRoles_FULL`** (extended view model)
  - Extends `tbl_AspNetRoles`
  - `string Facility_Name`
  - `IEnumerable<SelectListItem> Parent_List`
  - `int UserCount` (count of users with this role)
  - `string rolid`

- **`ApplicationRoleModel`** (ViewModel)
  - `string Id`
  - `bool Parent`
  - `string Name` (required)
  - `int UserCount`

- **`ApplicationRole`** (extends `IdentityUserRole`)
  - Minimal - primarily used for ASP.NET Identity integration

**Role Controller:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\RoleController.cs`
- Requires `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]`
- Uses raw SQL queries to fetch roles with user counts
- Example role names in code: `"BF Administrator"`, `"BF Manage User Password"`, `"BF User Manager (Training)"`, `"BF User Position Manage"`, `"BF User Position Add"`

---

### 3. **Permission Model/Entity Classes**

**Important Finding:** BeFirst Portal uses **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** via ASP.NET Identity. **There is NO explicit "Permission" entity/table.**

Instead:
- Permissions are managed implicitly through **roles** and **[Authorize(Roles="...")] attributes** on controller actions
- Example: `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF User Position Manage")]` on the `PositionController.Index()` action
- Role names ARE the permissions (e.g., "BF User Position Add" is both a role and a permission)

**Authorization Pattern:**
- Uses `System.Web.Mvc` `[Authorize]` attribute (MVC5)
- Role-based authorization: `[Authorize(Roles = "RoleName1,RoleName2")]`
- Claims-based identity available via `ClaimsIdentity` in `ApplicationUser.GenerateUserIdentityAsync()`

---

### 4. **Position Model/Entity Classes**

**Primary Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPosition.cs`

**Classes:**
- **`tbl_AspNetPosition`** (maps to `AspNetPosition` table)
  - `int PositionID` (primary key)
  - `string Position` (job title/position name)

- **`tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL`** (extended view model)
  - Extends `tbl_AspNetPosition`
  - No additional properties (currently empty extension)

- **`tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL_partial`** (partial ViewModel)
  - `IQueryable<tbl_AspNetPosition_FULL> Items`
  - `int id`

**Position Controller:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\PositionController.cs`
- Requires `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF User Position Manage,BF User Position Add")]`
- Uses raw SQL: `INSERT INTO [dbo].[AspNetPosition] (Position) VALUES (@Position)`
- Example positions stored as simple names like "Manager", "Technician", etc.

---

### 5. **Entity Relationships**

**Database Tables/Mappings:**

| Table | Maps To | Primary Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `AspNetUsers` | `ApplicationUser` | User accounts (ASP.NET Identity) |
| `AspNetRoles` | `ApplicationRole` (via `tbl_AspNetRoles`) | Role definitions |
| `AspNetUserRoles` | `tbl_AspNetUserRoles` | User ↔ Role mapping |
| `AspNetPosition` | `tbl_AspNetPosition` | Job positions |
| `AspNetUserPositions` | `tbl_AspNetUserPositions` | User ↔ Position mapping |
| `AspNetPositionRoles` | `tbl_AspNetPositionRoles` | Position ↔ Role mapping |

**Relationship Diagram:**
```
ApplicationUser (1) -------- (*) AspNetUserRoles (many) -------- (1) ApplicationRole
    Id                        UserId/RoleId                        Id

ApplicationUser (1) -------- (*) AspNetUserPositions (many) -------- (1) Position
    Id                        UserId/PositionId                     PositionID

Position (1) -------- (*) AspNetPositionRoles (many) -------- (1) ApplicationRole
    PositionID               PositionID/RoleID                      Id
```

---

### 6. **Key Relationship Classes**

**User-Role Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationRoleUsers.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetUserRoles")]
public class tbl_AspNetUserRoles
{
    public string UserId { get; set; }
    public string RoleId { get; set; }
    public int? Facility_Key { get; set; }  // Multi-facility support
}
```

**User-Position Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPositionUsers.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetUserPositions")]
public class tbl_AspNetUserPositions
{
    public int AutoId { get; set; }
    public string UserId { get; set; }
    public int? PositionId { get; set; }
}
```

**Position-Role Mapping:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationPositionRoles.cs`
```csharp
[Table("AspNetPositionRoles")]
public class tbl_AspNetPositionRoles
{
    public int AutoID { get; set; }
    public int? PositionID { get; set; }
    public string RolID { get; set; }  // Note: typo in "RolID"
}
```

---

### 7. **Existing Queries and Services**

**Service Layer:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\ApplicationUserService.cs`
- Extends `UserManager<ApplicationUser>`
- Provides: `GetById(userId)`, `GetByUserName(userName)`
- Uses `IUserStore<ApplicationUser>` for data access
- Configured via Autofac in `App_Start/DependencyRegister.cs`

**Query Examples from Controllers:**

**Role retrieval with user counts** (RoleController.Index):
```sql
SELECT [Id], [Name], [Facility_Key], [Parent], Count(AspNetUserRoles.UserId) AS UserCount
FROM AspNetRoles 
LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id
WHERE IsVisible=1 
GROUP BY [Id], [Name], [Facility_Key], [Parent]
ORDER BY Name ASC
```

**Position retrieval** (PositionController.Index):
```sql
SELECT [PositionID], [Position] FROM [AspNetPosition] ORDER BY Position
```

---

### 8. **Database Context**

**Primary Context:**
`S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Data\LegacyDataContext\DataContext.cs`
- Extends `IdentityDbContext<ApplicationUser>` (EF6)
- Supports raw SQL queries via `Database.SqlQuery<T>()`
- Uses transaction management for all saves

**File Paths Summary:**
- Models: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Account\*.cs`
- Controllers: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\*Controller.cs`
- Data context: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Framework\Data\LegacyDataContext\DataContext.cs`

---

### 9. **Multi-Tenancy/Multi-Facility Support**

The system supports multiple facilities:
- `AspNetRoles.Facility_Key` - Role restricted to specific facility
- `AspNetUserRoles.Facility_Key` - User-role assignment per facility
- User can have same role at different facilities with different constraints

---

### 10. **Authorization Pattern**

Uses standard ASP.NET Identity 2.0 with MVC5 Authorize attribute:
```csharp
[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]
public ActionResult Index() { }
```

No explicit permission checking—permissions are role names themselves.

Let me create a summary document with all the key findings: ## BeFirst_Portal Management Reports Exploration - Findings Report Based on my thorough exploration of the BeFirst_Portal codebase, here are the key findings about existing management reports and reporting patterns: ### 1. **Report Architecture Overview** The portal uses an **MVC Areas-based reporting pattern** with reports distributed across multiple areas: **Report Controllers Found:** - `Personnel/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - HR/bonus reports - `Manufacturing/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Production/equipment reports - `Materials/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Inventory reports - `Production/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Production summary reports - `Quality/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Quality audits and defect reports - `Engineering/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - NPI reports - `Rework/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Rework tracking - `Tickets/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` - Ticket reports **Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\{Area}\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` --- ### 2. **Report Implementation Patterns** **Typical Report Flow:** 1. Report view displays filter form (date ranges, dropdowns, employee IDs) 2. User clicks "Search" button 3. JavaScript AJAX call to controller method 4. Controller executes SQL query via `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()` 5. Partial view returns filtered data as HTML table or chart **Example: Personnel Bonus History Report** File: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` ```csharp [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF Efficiency Report")] public ActionResult BonusHistory() { return View(); } [AcceptVerbs(HttpVerbs.Get | HttpVerbs.Post)] public PartialViewResult GetBonusDataTable(DateTime stardate, DateTime enddate, int v_Type, string Gafete = "") { var model = new Bonus_History(); model.stardate = stardate; model.enddate = enddate.AddDays(+1); model.Gafete = Gafete; return PartialView("BonusHistory_Details", model); } ``` --- ### 3. **Existing HR/Admin-Related Reports** **Personnel Area Reports:** - **Bonus History Report** (`BonusHistory.cshtml`) - Date range filtering - Employee badge/Gafete filtering - Shows bonus calculations with color-coded efficiency percentages - Location: `/Personnel/Reports/BonusHistory` **Manage Area (Administration):** - **User Management** (`User/Index.cshtml`) - MvcGrid-based list view - Shows: UserName, Email, Badge, IsActive status - Filterable and sortable table - Edit/Print badge buttons per user - **Role Management** (`Role/Index.cshtml`) - MvcGrid-based list view - Shows: Role Name, User Count - Sortable and filterable - Edit functionality - **Position Management** (`Position/Index.cshtml`) - MvcGrid-based list view - Shows: PositionID, Position Name - Filterable and sortable --- ### 4. **Printable Report Patterns** **Key Styling Approach:** File: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Content\css\utilities.css` (line 3559-3563) ```css @media print { .noPrint { display: none; } } ``` **Report View Pattern for Printing:** File: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` - Section style tags hide headers/footers for print: ```html @section head{ } ``` - Simple HTML table with `border="1"` for printing - Dynamic cell coloring based on data values (e.g., efficiency bands: dw-0, dw-5, dw-15, dw-gray) --- ### 5. **Data Access Patterns** **Primary Method: Raw SQL via ADO.NET** Framework helper: `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()` Example from Manufacturing Reports: ```csharp SqlParameter[] parameters = new SqlParameter[] { new SqlParameter("@stardate", SqlDbType.DateTime) { Value = stardate}, new SqlParameter("@enddate", SqlDbType.DateTime) { Value = enddate }, }; DataTable dtResult = Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery( "SELECT ... FROM [table] WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN @stardate AND @enddate", parameters, "BeFirst:ConnectionString", 60 // timeout in seconds ); ``` **Export Patterns:** - Excel export via `EPPlus` (ExcelPackage) - Historically CSV export (commented out in some controllers) - Direct browser download via `File()` action result Example from Manufacturing SMTAOI_Export: ```csharp using (ExcelPackage excelPackage = new ExcelPackage()) { ExcelWorksheet worksheet = excelPackage.Workbook.Worksheets.Add("Sheet1"); // Write column headers and data rows... using (MemoryStream memoryStream = new MemoryStream()) { excelPackage.SaveAs(memoryStream); return File(memoryStream.ToArray(), "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "data.xlsx"); } } ``` --- ### 6. **UI Grid Framework: MvcGrid** **Library:** NonFactors.Mvc.Grid (installed via NuGet) **Features Used:** - Filterable (search by column) - Sortable (click column headers) - Pageable (customizable rows per page) - Action links (Edit, Delete, Print) - Empty state messages **Example from User Index:** File: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` ```html @(Html .Grid(Model) .Build(columns => { columns.Add(model => model.UserName) .RenderedAs(model => Html.ActionLink(model.UserName.ToString(), "Edit", "User", new { id = model.Id }, new { })); columns.Add(model => model.Name); columns.Add(model => model.Badge); columns.Add(model => model.IsActive); columns.Add(model => "Edit") .RenderedAs(y => Html.ActionLink("Edit", "Edit", new { id = y.Id }, new { @class = "editbutton" })) .Css("editlink"); }) .Empty("No data found") .Pageable(pager => { pager.PagesToDisplay = 10; pager.RowsPerPage = 15; }) .Filterable() .Sortable() ) ``` --- ### 7. **Layout & Styling Foundation** **Main Layout:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Views\Shared\_Layout.cshtml` **CSS Framework Stack:** - Bootstrap 5.3 - UIKit (custom UK classes for grid layout) - Font Awesome 6 (icons) - Custom `utilities.css` and `style.css` **Key CSS Classes:** - `.md-card` - Card container for reports - `.md-card-toolbar` - Report title header - `.md-card-content` - Main report content area - `.mvc-grid` - Grid wrapper (from MvcGrid library) - `.noPrint` - Hidden on print - `@media print` - Print-specific styles **JavaScript Libraries:** - jQuery 3.3.1 - jQuery UI (datepicker) - Chart.js (bar/line charts) - D3.js (data visualization) - Select2 (dropdown enhancement) --- ### 8. **Report Filter Pattern** **Standard Filter Row Structure:** ```html
@Html.Label("Start", new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" })
@Html.Editor("Start", "{0:MM/dd/yyyy}", new { htmlAttributes = new { @class = "form-control", id = "ddlDateStart" } })
Search
``` **Common Filter Controls:** - Date range pickers (jQuery UI datepicker) - Dropdowns (customer, area, station, shift, part number) - Text input (employee badge/ID) - Checkboxes (flags like "Ignore REV", "Kanban") --- ### 9. **Database Access Patterns for Reports** **Connection Strings (from CLAUDE.md):** - `BeFirst:ConnectionString` - Main BeFirst database - `BeFirst:ConnectionStringMachines` - Equipment/OEE data - `BeFirst:ConnectionStringHanwha` - SMT machine data **Common Report Queries:** - Join `AspNetUsers` or `ASPNETUSERS` for user data - Use `AspNetUserRoles` for role assignments - Use `AspNetRoles` for role definitions - Time-series data from `PROD_MACHINES_RESULTS` database - Quality data from `PROD_DWH` **SQL Parameter Pattern:** ```csharp SqlParameter[] parameters = new SqlParameter[] { new SqlParameter("@UserID", SqlDbType.NVarChar) { Value = userID }, new SqlParameter("@RoleID", SqlDbType.NVarChar) { Value = roleID }, new SqlParameter("@IsActive", SqlDbType.Int) { Value = 1 }, }; ``` --- ### 10. **Role-Based Authorization Patterns** **Decorator Pattern Used:** ```csharp [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF Efficiency Report")] public ActionResult BonusHistory() { ... } [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF Manage User Password")] public ActionResult List() { ... } ``` **Common Roles for Reports:** - `BF Administrator` - Full access - `BF Efficiency Report` - Specific report access - `BF User Manager (Training)` - User management - `BF Manage User Password` - Password resets --- ### 11. **Key Frameworks & NuGet Packages** From `Portal_BeFirst.csproj` analysis: - **EPPlus** (v4.x) - Excel generation - **NPOI** - Office document handling - **Autofac** - Dependency injection - **Entity Framework 6** - ORM for legacy code - **Entity Framework Core 3.1** - Newer contexts - **NonFactors.Mvc.Grid** - Grid/table rendering - **Newtonsoft.Json** - JSON serialization - **DocumentFormat.OpenXml** - Office XML - **AspNet.Identity** - User authentication - **Toastr** - Notifications - **TinyMCE** - Rich text editing --- ### 12. **File Locations Summary** | Type | Location | Pattern | |------|----------|---------| | Report Controllers | `Areas/{Area}/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` | Handles GET/POST, returns views/partials | | Report Views | `Areas/{Area}/Views/Reports/*.cshtml` | Main report container views | | Report Detail Partials | `Areas/{Area}/Views/Reports/*_Details.cshtml` | Data table/grid rendering | | Admin Views | `Areas/Manage/Views/{Entity}/*.cshtml` | User, Role, Position management | | Shared Layouts | `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml` | Main portal layout | | Shared Partials | `Views/Shared/MvcGrid/` | Grid rendering components | | CSS Styling | `Content/css/*.css` | style.css, utilities.css | | JavaScript | `Content/js/`, `Content/vendor/` | Scripts and third-party libs | --- ### 13. **Concrete Examples for Management Reports** **Path 1: User/Role Matrix Report (Similar Pattern)** - Controller: `Manage/Controllers/UserController.cs` (already has user list) - View: Could use similar MvcGrid pattern from `User/Index.cshtml` - Data access: Join `AspNetUsers`, `AspNetUserRoles`, `AspNetRoles` - Print styling: Hide header/footer like `BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` **Path 2: Audit/Access Log Report (Like Production Reports)** - Controller: New `ReportsController` in Personnel or IS area - View structure: Filter form + partial for results - Data: Query audit tables with date ranges - Export: EPPlus Excel export like `Manufacturing SMTAOI_Export` **Path 3: Simple List Report (Like Role/User Index)** - Use `Html.Grid()` with NonFactors.Mvc.Grid - Built-in filtering and sorting - Add print button triggering `window.print()` --- ### 14. **Key Observations** 1. **No dedicated reporting engine** - Reports are built ad-hoc in each area using raw SQL + views 2. **Dual approach**: Some reports use `.Grid()` helper (Manage area), others use raw DataTable rendering (Production, Manufacturing) 3. **Print support is basic** - CSS hides elements via `@media print` rule in each view 4. **Database-heavy** - Most filtering/aggregation done in SQL, not in C# 5. **Authorization is role-based** - `[Authorize(Roles="...")]` attributes protect access 6. **No centralized report dashboard** - Reports scattered across areas with no unified UI 7. **Excel export exists** - Using EPPlus for some reports, but not consistent across all 8. **No report scheduling** - All reports are on-demand, user-triggered 9. **Limited UI consistency** - Date pickers, dropdowns styles vary between areas --- ### 15. **Recommended Approaches for New Management Reports** **For User/Role/Permission Reports:** 1. **Use the Manage Area** - Leverage existing `UserController`, `RoleController` infrastructure 2. **Use MvcGrid** - Similar to `User/Index.cshtml` and `Role/Index.cshtml` for consistency 3. **Follow SQL pattern** - Use `GetDatatableFromQuery()` for data access with parameterized queries 4. **Print-friendly view** - Add section with `@media print` styles, hide header/footer 5. **Authorization** - Add `[Authorize(Roles="BF Administrator,...")]` attribute 6. **Export** - Optionally add EPPlus Excel export like Manufacturing reports 7. **Location** - Create new controller method in `Manage/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` (if needed) or add to existing controller --- ## Files to Reference When Building New Reports **Master References:** 1. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory.cshtml` - Full report form pattern 2. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` - Table/data rendering + print styles 3. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manufacturing\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` - Complex SQL patterns 4. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` - MvcGrid pattern for admin lists 5. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\CLAUDE.md` - Architecture overview 6. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Views\Shared\_Layout.cshtml` - Main layout structure 7. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Content\css\utilities.css` - Print media queries

FROM [table] WHERE TrnDate BETWEEN @stardate AND @enddate", parameters, "BeFirst:ConnectionString", 60 // timeout in seconds ); ``` **Export Patterns:** - Excel export via `EPPlus` (ExcelPackage) - Historically CSV export (commented out in some controllers) - Direct browser download via `File()` action result Example from Manufacturing SMTAOI_Export: ```csharp using (ExcelPackage excelPackage = new ExcelPackage()) { ExcelWorksheet worksheet = excelPackage.Workbook.Worksheets.Add("Sheet1"); // Write column headers and data rows... using (MemoryStream memoryStream = new MemoryStream()) { excelPackage.SaveAs(memoryStream); return File(memoryStream.ToArray(), "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet", "data.xlsx"); } } ``` --- ### 6. **UI Grid Framework: MvcGrid** **Library:** NonFactors.Mvc.Grid (installed via NuGet) **Features Used:** - Filterable (search by column) - Sortable (click column headers) - Pageable (customizable rows per page) - Action links (Edit, Delete, Print) - Empty state messages **Example from User Index:** File: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` ```html @(Html .Grid(Model) .Build(columns => { columns.Add(model => model.UserName) .RenderedAs(model => Html.ActionLink(model.UserName.ToString(), "Edit", "User", new { id = model.Id }, new { })); columns.Add(model => model.Name); columns.Add(model => model.Badge); columns.Add(model => model.IsActive); columns.Add(model => "Edit") .RenderedAs(y => Html.ActionLink("Edit", "Edit", new { id = y.Id }, new { @class = "editbutton" })) .Css("editlink"); }) .Empty("No data found") .Pageable(pager => { pager.PagesToDisplay = 10; pager.RowsPerPage = 15; }) .Filterable() .Sortable() ) ``` --- ### 7. **Layout & Styling Foundation** **Main Layout:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Views\Shared\_Layout.cshtml` **CSS Framework Stack:** - Bootstrap 5.3 - UIKit (custom UK classes for grid layout) - Font Awesome 6 (icons) - Custom `utilities.css` and `style.css` **Key CSS Classes:** - `.md-card` - Card container for reports - `.md-card-toolbar` - Report title header - `.md-card-content` - Main report content area - `.mvc-grid` - Grid wrapper (from MvcGrid library) - `.noPrint` - Hidden on print - `@media print` - Print-specific styles **JavaScript Libraries:** - jQuery 3.3.1 - jQuery UI (datepicker) - Chart.js (bar/line charts) - D3.js (data visualization) - Select2 (dropdown enhancement) --- ### 8. **Report Filter Pattern** **Standard Filter Row Structure:** ```html
@Html.Label("Start", new { @class = "m-0 fs-14" })
@Html.Editor("Start", "{0:MM/dd/yyyy}", new { htmlAttributes = new { @class = "form-control", id = "ddlDateStart" } })
Search
``` **Common Filter Controls:** - Date range pickers (jQuery UI datepicker) - Dropdowns (customer, area, station, shift, part number) - Text input (employee badge/ID) - Checkboxes (flags like "Ignore REV", "Kanban") --- ### 9. **Database Access Patterns for Reports** **Connection Strings (from CLAUDE.md):** - `BeFirst:ConnectionString` - Main BeFirst database - `BeFirst:ConnectionStringMachines` - Equipment/OEE data - `BeFirst:ConnectionStringHanwha` - SMT machine data **Common Report Queries:** - Join `AspNetUsers` or `ASPNETUSERS` for user data - Use `AspNetUserRoles` for role assignments - Use `AspNetRoles` for role definitions - Time-series data from `PROD_MACHINES_RESULTS` database - Quality data from `PROD_DWH` **SQL Parameter Pattern:** ```csharp SqlParameter[] parameters = new SqlParameter[] { new SqlParameter("@UserID", SqlDbType.NVarChar) { Value = userID }, new SqlParameter("@RoleID", SqlDbType.NVarChar) { Value = roleID }, new SqlParameter("@IsActive", SqlDbType.Int) { Value = 1 }, }; ``` --- ### 10. **Role-Based Authorization Patterns** **Decorator Pattern Used:** ```csharp [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF Efficiency Report")] public ActionResult BonusHistory() { ... } [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator,BF Manage User Password")] public ActionResult List() { ... } ``` **Common Roles for Reports:** - `BF Administrator` - Full access - `BF Efficiency Report` - Specific report access - `BF User Manager (Training)` - User management - `BF Manage User Password` - Password resets --- ### 11. **Key Frameworks & NuGet Packages** From `Portal_BeFirst.csproj` analysis: - **EPPlus** (v4.x) - Excel generation - **NPOI** - Office document handling - **Autofac** - Dependency injection - **Entity Framework 6** - ORM for legacy code - **Entity Framework Core 3.1** - Newer contexts - **NonFactors.Mvc.Grid** - Grid/table rendering - **Newtonsoft.Json** - JSON serialization - **DocumentFormat.OpenXml** - Office XML - **AspNet.Identity** - User authentication - **Toastr** - Notifications - **TinyMCE** - Rich text editing --- ### 12. **File Locations Summary** | Type | Location | Pattern | |------|----------|---------| | Report Controllers | `Areas/{Area}/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` | Handles GET/POST, returns views/partials | | Report Views | `Areas/{Area}/Views/Reports/*.cshtml` | Main report container views | | Report Detail Partials | `Areas/{Area}/Views/Reports/*_Details.cshtml` | Data table/grid rendering | | Admin Views | `Areas/Manage/Views/{Entity}/*.cshtml` | User, Role, Position management | | Shared Layouts | `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml` | Main portal layout | | Shared Partials | `Views/Shared/MvcGrid/` | Grid rendering components | | CSS Styling | `Content/css/*.css` | style.css, utilities.css | | JavaScript | `Content/js/`, `Content/vendor/` | Scripts and third-party libs | --- ### 13. **Concrete Examples for Management Reports** **Path 1: User/Role Matrix Report (Similar Pattern)** - Controller: `Manage/Controllers/UserController.cs` (already has user list) - View: Could use similar MvcGrid pattern from `User/Index.cshtml` - Data access: Join `AspNetUsers`, `AspNetUserRoles`, `AspNetRoles` - Print styling: Hide header/footer like `BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` **Path 2: Audit/Access Log Report (Like Production Reports)** - Controller: New `ReportsController` in Personnel or IS area - View structure: Filter form + partial for results - Data: Query audit tables with date ranges - Export: EPPlus Excel export like `Manufacturing SMTAOI_Export` **Path 3: Simple List Report (Like Role/User Index)** - Use `Html.Grid()` with NonFactors.Mvc.Grid - Built-in filtering and sorting - Add print button triggering `window.print()` --- ### 14. **Key Observations** 1. **No dedicated reporting engine** - Reports are built ad-hoc in each area using raw SQL + views 2. **Dual approach**: Some reports use `.Grid()` helper (Manage area), others use raw DataTable rendering (Production, Manufacturing) 3. **Print support is basic** - CSS hides elements via `@media print` rule in each view 4. **Database-heavy** - Most filtering/aggregation done in SQL, not in C# 5. **Authorization is role-based** - `[Authorize(Roles="...")]` attributes protect access 6. **No centralized report dashboard** - Reports scattered across areas with no unified UI 7. **Excel export exists** - Using EPPlus for some reports, but not consistent across all 8. **No report scheduling** - All reports are on-demand, user-triggered 9. **Limited UI consistency** - Date pickers, dropdowns styles vary between areas --- ### 15. **Recommended Approaches for New Management Reports** **For User/Role/Permission Reports:** 1. **Use the Manage Area** - Leverage existing `UserController`, `RoleController` infrastructure 2. **Use MvcGrid** - Similar to `User/Index.cshtml` and `Role/Index.cshtml` for consistency 3. **Follow SQL pattern** - Use `GetDatatableFromQuery()` for data access with parameterized queries 4. **Print-friendly view** - Add section with `@media print` styles, hide header/footer 5. **Authorization** - Add `[Authorize(Roles="BF Administrator,...")]` attribute 6. **Export** - Optionally add EPPlus Excel export like Manufacturing reports 7. **Location** - Create new controller method in `Manage/Controllers/ReportsController.cs` (if needed) or add to existing controller --- ## Files to Reference When Building New Reports **Master References:** 1. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory.cshtml` - Full report form pattern 2. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` - Table/data rendering + print styles 3. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manufacturing\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` - Complex SQL patterns 4. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` - MvcGrid pattern for admin lists 5. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\CLAUDE.md` - Architecture overview 6. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Views\Shared\_Layout.cshtml` - Main layout structure 7. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Content\css\utilities.css` - Print media queries

content.thinking:
Excellent! I now have comprehensive information from all three exploration agents. Let me synthesize the findings:

**From Agent 1 (Management Reports Patterns):**
- Reports are distributed across areas with area-specific ReportsControllers
- Two main patterns: MvcGrid (Manage area) and raw SQL + DataTable (Manufacturing, Personnel)
- Printable reports use `@media print` CSS to hide headers/footers
- Data access via `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()` with parameterized SQL
- No centralized reporting dashboard
- Examples: Personnel/BonusHistory, Manage/User/Index, Manage/Role/Index, Manufacturing reports
- MvcGrid for filterable/sortable admin lists
- EPPlus for Excel export in some reports

**From Agent 2 (User/Role/Permission Model):**
- ASP.NET Identity 2.0 framework
- ApplicationUser (extends IdentityUser) with custom properties: Name, Surname, Badge, IsActive, ControlID, PictureId
- Roles via AspNetRoles table with 100+ roles following "BF [Area] [Function]" pattern
- No explicit "Permission" entity - permissions ARE roles
- Position model via AspNetPosition table (PositionID, Position name)
- Relationships: AspNetUserRoles (User ↔ Role), AspNetUserPositions (User ↔ Position), AspNetPositionRoles (Position ↔ Role)
- Authorization via [Authorize(Roles="...")] attributes
- Multi-facility support via Facility_Key
- Controllers: UserController, RoleController, PositionController in Manage area

**From Agent 3 (Database Schema):**
- ASP.NET Identity tables: AspNetUsers, AspNetRoles, AspNetUserRoles
- Position tables: AspNetPosition, AspNetUserPositions, AspNetPositionRoles


- Azure AD authentication (OAuth2/OIDC) and 100+ roles documented in knowledge-base
- MES database likely contains Personnel/Team tables
- RBAC implemented entirely through roles with no separate permissions table

Now moving to Phase 2 design. The requirements are clear: a management report showing role membership across users, and another identifying users with assigned permissions but no position. Given the existing patterns, this should fit naturally into the Manage area as either a new ReportsController or an extension of the existing admin views, leveraging the same MvcGrid and SQL query patterns already established.

input.prompt:
Design the implementation plan for a new management report in BeFirst_Portal that shows:
1. Roles and their user members
2. Users with permissions (roles) assigned but without a position

## Context from Exploration

### Existing Report Patterns
- Reports live in area-specific `ReportsController.cs` files
- Two patterns: **MvcGrid** (Manage area for admin lists) and **raw SQL + DataTable** (Personnel/Manufacturing)
- Printable reports use `@media print` CSS hiding headers/footers
- Data access via `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()`
- Example files:
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` (MvcGrid pattern)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Role\Index.cshtml` (MvcGrid with UserCount)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory.cshtml` (printable report with filters)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` (SQL queries)

### Data Model (ASP.NET Identity 2.0)
**Tables:**
- `AspNetUsers` → ApplicationUser (Id, UserName, Email, Badge, IsActive, Name, Surname)
- `AspNetRoles` → ApplicationRole (Id, Name, Facility_Key, Parent)
- `AspNetUserRoles` (UserId, RoleId, Facility_Key) - User ↔ Role junction
- `AspNetPosition` (PositionID, Position) - Job positions
- `AspNetUserPositions` (UserId, PositionId) - User ↔ Position junction
- `AspNetPositionRoles` (PositionID, RolID) - Position ↔ Role junction

**Authorization:** `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]` required for Manage area controllers

**Controllers:**
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\UserController.cs`
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\RoleController.cs`
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\PositionController.cs`

### Key Requirements
1. Management needs to **review and print** roles with their members
2. Management needs to see users with roles but **no position assigned**
3. Report should be accessible to management (likely BF Administrator role)
4. Printable format

## Design Questions to Answer

1. **Location:** Should this be a new `ReportsController` in Manage area or add action to existing controller?
2. **Pattern:** Single report with two sections OR two separate reports?
3. **UI Approach:** MvcGrid (interactive filtering) OR static printable table (like BonusHistory)?
4. **Data Access:** Raw SQL queries (flexible, performant) OR Entity Framework (cleaner, type-safe)?
5. **Filters:** Any filters needed (active users only, specific roles, facility)?
6. **Export:** Excel export needed or just print-to-PDF via browser?

## Provide Implementation Plan

Design the recommended approach including:
- File locations and new files needed
- Controller actions and authorization
- SQL queries for both report sections
- View structure and styling approach
- Print styling strategy
- Trade-offs of your recommendations

Design the implementation plan for a new management report in BeFirst_Portal that shows:
1. Roles and their user members
2. Users with permissions (roles) assigned but without a position

## Context from Exploration

### Existing Report Patterns
- Reports live in area-specific `ReportsController.cs` files
- Two patterns: **MvcGrid** (Manage area for admin lists) and **raw SQL + DataTable** (Personnel/Manufacturing)
- Printable reports use `@media print` CSS hiding headers/footers
- Data access via `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()`
- Example files:
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\User\Index.cshtml` (MvcGrid pattern)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Role\Index.cshtml` (MvcGrid with UserCount)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory.cshtml` (printable report with filters)
  - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` (SQL queries)

### Data Model (ASP.NET Identity 2.0)
**Tables:**
- `AspNetUsers` → ApplicationUser (Id, UserName, Email, Badge, IsActive, Name, Surname)
- `AspNetRoles` → ApplicationRole (Id, Name, Facility_Key, Parent)
- `AspNetUserRoles` (UserId, RoleId, Facility_Key) - User ↔ Role junction
- `AspNetPosition` (PositionID, Position) - Job positions
- `AspNetUserPositions` (UserId, PositionId) - User ↔ Position junction
- `AspNetPositionRoles` (PositionID, RolID) - Position ↔ Role junction

**Authorization:** `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]` required for Manage area controllers

**Controllers:**
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\UserController.cs`
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\RoleController.cs`
- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\PositionController.cs`

### Key Requirements
1. Management needs to **review and print** roles with their members
2. Management needs to see users with roles but **no position assigned**
3. Report should be accessible to management (likely BF Administrator role)
4. Printable format

## Design Questions to Answer

1. **Location:** Should this be a new `ReportsController` in Manage area or add action to existing controller?
2. **Pattern:** Single report with two sections OR two separate reports?
3. **UI Approach:** MvcGrid (interactive filtering) OR static printable table (like BonusHistory)?
4. **Data Access:** Raw SQL queries (flexible, performant) OR Entity Framework (cleaner, type-safe)?
5. **Filters:** Any filters needed (active users only, specific roles, facility)?
6. **Export:** Excel export needed or just print-to-PDF via browser?

## Provide Implementation Plan

Design the recommended approach including:
- File locations and new files needed
- Controller actions and authorization
- SQL queries for both report sections
- View structure and styling approach
- Print styling strategy
- Trade-offs of your recommendations

Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding. Let me create a comprehensive implementation plan.

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN: Role Audit & Membership Report

### Overview
Based on my exploration of the BeFirst_Portal codebase, I've designed a comprehensive implementation plan for the management report that displays roles with members and identifies users with roles but no position.

### Architecture Analysis

**Existing Patterns Identified:**

1. **Report Location Precedent**: Personnel area has `ReportsController` with printable reports (BonusHistory)
2. **Data Access Pattern**: Raw SQL queries + DataTable manipulation (not ORM-heavy)
3. **View Strategy**: Two patterns observed:
   - **MvcGrid** (interactive, admin lists) - used in Manage area
   - **Printable tables** (static, report-ready) - used in Personnel area
4. **Authorization**: Manage area uses `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]`
5. **Database Schema**:
   - `AspNetRoles` (Id, Name, Facility_Key)
   - `AspNetUsers` (Id, UserName, Name, Surname, Badge, IsActive)
   - `AspNetUserRoles` (UserId, RoleId, Facility_Key) - junction table
   - `AspNetUserPositions` (UserId, PositionId) - links users to positions
   - `AspNetPosition` (PositionID, Position) - job titles

### Design Decisions

**1. Location: Create new ReportsController in Manage area**
- **Rationale**: Roles management is an admin/security concern (Manage area)
- **Precedent**: Role editing is in Manage/RoleController; reporting should follow
- **Path**: `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs`

**2. Pattern: Hybrid approach (single report, two distinct sections)**
- **Rationale**: Both queries are related (security audit) and should be on one page for printing
- **Alternative considered**: Two separate reports (would require managing two routes/views)
- **Chosen**: Single report with collapsible sections for "Roles & Members" and "Users Without Positions"

**3. UI Approach: Printable report with MvcGrid sections**
- **Rationale**: 
  - Management prints this report (stated requirement)
  - Printable format requires hiding headers/footers (established pattern in BonusHistory)
  - Section 1 needs hierarchical layout (roles → members) → use MvcGrid with grouping
  - Section 2 is simple flat list → basic table
- **Print styling**: Use `@media print` to hide navigation elements (like BonusHistory_Details)

**4. Data Access: Raw SQL queries**
- **Rationale**:
  - Consistent with existing PositionController and ReportsController pattern
  - Position controller uses raw SQL for complex queries (lines 206, 227)
  - Avoids multiple context calls
  - More performant for reports with multiple JOINs
- **Helper method approach**: Extract SQL to private methods for reusability

**5. Filters**:
- Active users only (checkbox, default: checked) - users often forget to deactivate
- Specific facility (dropdown) - multi-facility support visible in schema
- Optional: role name prefix filter (for partial role searches)

**6. Export**: Print-to-PDF via browser (no Excel export initially)
- **Rationale**: Established pattern; management can use browser "Print to PDF"
- **Alternative**: Could add NPOI Excel export later (used in UserController.UploadFile)

### SQL Queries

**Query 1: Roles with Member Counts**
```sql
SELECT 
  AspNetRoles.Id,
  AspNetRoles.Name,
  AspNetRoles.Facility_Key,
  COUNT(AspNetUserRoles.UserId) AS MemberCount
FROM AspNetRoles
LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id
WHERE AspNetRoles.IsVisible = 1
GROUP BY AspNetRoles.Id, AspNetRoles.Name, AspNetRoles.Facility_Key
ORDER BY AspNetRoles.Name ASC
```

**Query 2: Role Members (detail)**
```sql
SELECT 
  AspNetUserRoles.RoleId,
  AspNetUserRoles.UserId,
  AspNetUsers.Name,
  AspNetUsers.Surname,
  AspNetUsers.Badge,
  AspNetUsers.UserName,
  AspNetUsers.IsActive
FROM AspNetUserRoles
INNER JOIN AspNetUsers ON AspNetUserRoles.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id
WHERE AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = @RoleId
  AND AspNetUsers.IsActive = 1 (conditional)
ORDER BY AspNetUsers.Name ASC
```

**Query 3: Users with Roles but No Position**
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
  AspNetUsers.Id,
  AspNetUsers.Name,
  AspNetUsers.Surname,
  AspNetUsers.Badge,
  AspNetUsers.UserName,
  AspNetUsers.IsActive,
  STRING_AGG(AspNetRoles.Name, ', ') AS RoleNames
FROM AspNetUsers
INNER JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id
INNER JOIN AspNetRoles ON AspNetRoles.Id = AspNetUserRoles.RoleId
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM AspNetUserPositions 
  WHERE AspNetUserPositions.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id
)
  AND AspNetUsers.IsActive = 1 (conditional)
GROUP BY AspNetUsers.Id, AspNetUsers.Name, AspNetUsers.Surname, AspNetUsers.Badge, AspNetUsers.UserName, AspNetUsers.IsActive
ORDER BY AspNetUsers.Name ASC
```

### File Structure & Implementation Steps

**Phase 1: Controller Setup**
- Create `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs`
- Add `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]` class decorator
- Create action methods:
  - `Index()` - GET, returns view with filter form
  - `GetRolesWithMembers()` - POST, returns partial with role/member grid
  - `GetUsersWithoutPosition()` - POST, returns partial with user table

**Phase 2: View Structure**
- Create `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\Index.cshtml`
  - Filters section: facility dropdown, "active only" checkbox, search button
  - Two collapsible divs: `#RolesMembersPreview` and `#UsersNoPositionPreview`
  - Print styling (hide header/footer like BonusHistory)

- Create `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\RolesWithMembers.cshtml` (partial)
  - MvcGrid with columns: [RoleName] | [MemberCount] | [Members List]
  - Expandable rows or nested grid showing members
  - Alternative: Grouped table with role headers and member rows

- Create `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\UsersWithoutPosition.cshtml` (partial)
  - Simple table: Name | Badge | Username | Active | Assigned Roles
  - Sortable by name/badge

**Phase 3: Models (optional, for clarity)**
- Create simple DTOs in `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Models\Reports\`:
  - `RoleWithMembersModel` (RoleId, Name, MemberCount, Members List)
  - `UserWithoutPositionModel` (UserId, Name, Badge, Username, IsActive, Roles)

**Phase 4: Styling**
- Printable CSS (`@media print`):
  - Hide navbar, sidebar, action buttons
  - Set page breaks between roles
  - Optimize table widths
  - Add "Page X of Y" headers

### Trade-offs & Rationale

| Decision | Pro | Con | Rationale |
|----------|-----|-----|-----------|
| **Manage area** | Security-focused; admin-only | Slightly different from Personnel reports | Roles = security = Manage domain |
| **Single report** | One print/view; related data | Slightly complex UI | Audit workflow expects them together |
| **Raw SQL** | Performance; matches codebase | Manual SQL; no validation | Consistency with existing reports |
| **MvcGrid** | Interactive filtering; familiar | More complex than static table | Management may filter by facility/role |
| **No Excel export** | Simpler MVP; browser handles PDF | Users must save as PDF manually | Meets stated "printable" requirement |
| **Active users only** | Cleaner report; avoids noise | Need toggle for historical audit | Checkbox gives flexibility |

### Critical Implementation Notes

1. **SQL Server STRING_AGG()**: Requires SQL Server 2017+. If older version, use `FOR XML PATH('')` approach instead:
```sql
STUFF((SELECT ', ' + AspNetRoles.Name 
       FROM AspNetRoles 
       INNER JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id 
       WHERE AspNetUserRoles.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id 
       FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE).value('.', 'NVARCHAR(MAX)'), 1, 2, '')
```

2. **Facility_Key handling**: Schema supports multi-facility. Report should:
   - Filter by selected facility (or "All Facilities")
   - Show facility in output when viewing all

3. **Authorization**: `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]` - may want to add `"BF User Manager"` roles if they exist

4. **Performance**: 
   - Role → Members LEFT JOIN: Could be slow with many users
   - Consider pagination or filtering by facility first
   - Add SQL indexes on AspNetUserRoles(RoleId, UserId) if not present

5. **Print preview**: Test with browser print preview (Ctrl+P) to ensure layout is clean

- `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` - Core logic with SQL queries

# Management Report: Roles & User Membership Audit ## Context Management requires a comprehensive report for auditing user access and role assignments. The report must show: 1. All roles with their assigned user members 2. Users who have role assignments but no position assigned (potential security/HR concern) This report addresses two management needs: - **Access control audit**: Who has which permissions (via roles)? - **Position compliance**: Are all users with system access properly assigned to positions? The report must be printable for management review and compliance documentation. ## Implementation Approach ### Location: Manage Area ReportsController **Create new:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` **Rationale:** - Roles and user management already live in Manage area (RoleController, UserController) - Security/audit reports belong with security management features - Follows established area organization pattern ### Pattern: Single Report with Two Sections **Report structure:** 1. Main view with optional filters (active users only, facility filter) 2. Section 1: Roles with member lists (hierarchical display) 3. Section 2: Users without positions (flat table) 4. Print-friendly styling (hide headers/footers via `@media print`) **Rationale:** - Both queries relate to the same audit workflow - Single page is easier to print and review together - Follows Personnel/BonusHistory report pattern (filter form + results) ### Data Access: Raw SQL with Parameterized Queries **Framework method:** `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()` **Rationale:** - Consistent with existing RoleController and ReportsController patterns - RoleController already has similar query (roles with user counts) at line 30-39 - Performance advantage for reporting queries with multiple JOINs - Established pattern in codebase (no need to introduce new approaches) ### Authorization **Decorator:** `[Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")]` on controller class **Rationale:** - Matches RoleController authorization pattern - Report contains sensitive access control information - Could extend to other admin roles if needed (BF User Manager) ## Critical Files **Files to create:** 1. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` 2. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\Index.cshtml` 3. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\_RolesWithMembers.cshtml` (partial) 4. `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Reports\_UsersWithoutPosition.cshtml` (partial) **Files to reference:** - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\RoleController.cs` (lines 30-39, 183-196) - Query patterns - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` - Controller structure - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory.cshtml` - Filter form and layout - `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Personnel\Views\Reports\BonusHistory_Details.cshtml` - Print styling ## Implementation Steps ### Step 1: Create ReportsController **File:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Controllers\ReportsController.cs` **Actions to implement:** ```csharp [Authorize(Roles = "BF Administrator")] public class ReportsController : BaseSecurityController { private readonly DataContext db; public ReportsController() { db = new DataContext(); } // Main report page with filters public ActionResult Index() { return View(); } // Section 1: Roles with members [AcceptVerbs(HttpVerbs.Get | HttpVerbs.Post)] public PartialViewResult GetRolesWithMembers(bool activeOnly = true) // Section 2: Users without positions [AcceptVerbs(HttpVerbs.Get | HttpVerbs.Post)] public PartialViewResult GetUsersWithoutPosition(bool activeOnly = true) } ``` ### Step 2: SQL Queries **Query 1: Roles with User Counts** ```sql SELECT AspNetRoles.Id, AspNetRoles.Name, AspNetRoles.Facility_Key, COUNT(AspNetUserRoles.UserId) AS MemberCount FROM AspNetRoles LEFT JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id WHERE AspNetRoles.IsVisible = 1 GROUP BY AspNetRoles.Id, AspNetRoles.Name, AspNetRoles.Facility_Key ORDER BY AspNetRoles.Name ASC ``` **Query 2: Role Members Detail** (called per role) ```sql SELECT AspNetUserRoles.RoleId, AspNetUserRoles.UserId, AspNetUsers.Name, AspNetUsers.Surname, AspNetUsers.Badge, AspNetUsers.UserName, AspNetUsers.IsActive FROM AspNetUserRoles INNER JOIN AspNetUsers ON AspNetUserRoles.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id WHERE AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = @RoleId AND (@ActiveOnly = 0 OR AspNetUsers.IsActive = 1) ORDER BY AspNetUsers.Name ASC ``` **Query 3: Users Without Positions** ```sql SELECT DISTINCT AspNetUsers.Id, AspNetUsers.Name, AspNetUsers.Surname, AspNetUsers.Badge, AspNetUsers.UserName, AspNetUsers.IsActive, STUFF(( SELECT ', ' + AspNetRoles.Name FROM AspNetRoles INNER JOIN AspNetUserRoles ON AspNetUserRoles.RoleId = AspNetRoles.Id WHERE AspNetUserRoles.UserId = AspNetUsers.Id FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE ).value('.', 'NVARCHAR(MAX)'), 1, 2, '') AS RoleNames FROM AspNetUsers.Id ) AND (@ActiveOnly = 0 OR AspNetUsers.IsActive = 1) ORDER BY AspNetUsers.Name ASC ``` **Note:** Using `FOR XML PATH` for concatenation (SQL Server 2016 compatible). ` - Results container 2: `

  • @Html.ActionLink("Role Audit Report", "Index", "Reports", new { area = "Manage" }, null)
    • ``` ## Key Database Tables - `AspNetUsers` - User accounts (Id, UserName, Name, Surname, Badge, IsActive) - `AspNetRoles` - Role definitions (Id, Name, Facility_Key, IsVisible) - `AspNetUserRoles` - User-to-Role assignments (UserId, RoleId, Facility_Key) - `AspNetPosition` - Position definitions (PositionID, Position) - `AspNetUserPositions` - User-to-Position assignments (UserId, PositionId) ## Verification **Manual testing:** 1. Log in as user with "BF Administrator" role 2. Navigate to Manage → Reports → Index (or direct URL: `/Manage/Reports`) 3. Click "Generate Report" button 4. Test with different SQL Server versions if needed (verify XML PATH concatenation works) **User acceptance:** - Management can review complete role membership - Management can identify users needing position assignments - Report can be printed/saved as PDF for compliance records ## Trade-offs | Decision | Benefits | Drawbacks | Mitigation | |----------|----------|-----------|------------| | **Manage area** | Natural location for security/admin reports | Slightly isolated from Personnel reports | Keep consistent styling | | **Single report** | Easier to print together; related audit data | Slightly complex UI | Clear section headers | | **Raw SQL** | Performance; matches codebase patterns | Manual parameter handling | Use SqlParameter properly | | **No pagination** | Simpler to print | Slow with 1000+ users | Filter by active only (default) | | **Basic print CSS** | Quick implementation | Not as polished as professional reports | Good enough for compliance | | **No Excel export** | Simpler MVP | Users must print-to-PDF | Can add later if requested | ## Performance Considerations - Query 1 (roles with counts): Fast, ~100 roles - Query 2 (members per role): Called per role, could be slow if rendering all roles expanded - Query 3 (users without positions): Fast with proper indexes on AspNetUserPositions - **Recommendation:** Render Query 2 on-demand (expand/collapse per role) for better performance ## Security Considerations - Report shows sensitive access control information - Restricted to "BF Administrator" role only - No sensitive data (passwords, tokens) exposed - Audit log should record when report is accessed (if audit logging exists) ## Future Enhancements (Not in MVP) - Excel export (EPPlus) - Facility filter (if multi-facility matters) - Date range filter (show role assignments added/removed in period) - Position recommendations (suggest positions based on role patterns) - Email report to management on schedule - Pagination for large user/role lists

Users who have role assignments but no position assigned (potential security/HR concern) This report addresses two management needs: - **Access control audit**: Who has which permissions (via roles)? - **Position compliance**: Are all users with system access properly assigned to positions? Main view with optional filters (active users only, facility filter) 2. Section 1: Roles with member lists (hierarchical display) 3. Section 2: Users without positions (flat table) 4. Print-friendly styling (hide headers/footers via `@media print`) **Rationale:** - Both queries relate to the same audit workflow - Single page is easier to print and review together - Follows Personnel/BonusHistory report pattern (filter form + results) ### Data Access: Raw SQL with Parameterized Queries **Framework method:** `Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetDatatableFromQuery()`
